history.title: History
history.emptySubtitle: Your past crop recommendations will appear here once you analyse your farm.
history.disclaimer: Recommendations are AI-assisted. Always consult a local agronomist.

onboarding.slide1Desc: AI-powered crop recommendations using real soil and weather data — built for Rwandan farmers.
onboarding.slide2Desc: Our ML model analyses satellite soil data, live weather, and your GPS location to suggest the best crops.
onboarding.slide3Desc: Get detailed planting guides with animated instructions for every recommended crop.
onboarding.slide4Desc: Use CropVana in English or Kinyarwanda. Switch anytime from your profile.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.heroSub: AI-powered crop recommendations from real satellite and weather data
howItWorks.accuracySub: Trained on 2,200 crop-soil samples across 22 crop types
howItWorks.step1Desc: The app reads your exact coordinates using your phone's GPS — no manual input needed.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Satellite-derived Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium, and pH values for your exact location from the iSDAsoil API.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Live temperature and humidity from OpenWeather, combined with seasonal rainfall from NASA POWER.
howItWorks.step4Desc: A Random Forest model trained on 2,200 samples predicts the top 3 crops with confidence scores.
howItWorks.step5Desc: Results are enriched with planting calendar, agronomic reasoning, and fertilizer advice in your language.
howItWorks.sourceIsda: Satellite soil maps at 30m resolution — N, P, K, pH for all of Africa
howItWorks.sourceOw: Real-time temperature and humidity from 40,000+ weather stations worldwide
howItWorks.sourceNasa: 30-year seasonal rainfall averages from NASA's POWER climate database
howItWorks.datasetNote: ML model trained on the Crop Recommendation Dataset (Kaggle). Note: dataset is India-origin; recommendations are calibrated for Rwanda's climate context.

plantingSeasons.maize: Season A or B — plant at start of rains
plantingSeasons.kidneybeans: Season A or B — well-drained soils
plantingSeasons.mango: Plant at start of rains — fruit in 3–5 years
plantingSeasons.watermelon: Season B (Mar–Jun) — needs warm dry weather
plantingSeasons.potato: Season A or B — cool highlands preferred
plantingSeasons.soybean: Season A or B — improves soil nitrogen
plantingSeasons.sweetpotato: Season A or B — ready in 3–5 months
plantingSeasons.tomato: Season A or B — warm, sheltered site

rice.requirements: Flooded paddy fields · Temp 22–32°C · pH 5.5–6.5 · High water supply
steps: Level the paddy field and build earthen bunds to hold 5–10 cm of water
Soak seeds 24 h, drain, then pre-germinate 2–3 days until tiny sprouts appear
Transplant 3–4 week-old seedlings 20 cm apart in rows spaced 25 cm
Maintain 5–10 cm water depth; apply DAP at planting, urea at tillering
Weed at weeks 2 and 6; monitor for blast disease and stem borers
Drain field 2 weeks before harvest; harvest when 85 % of grains are golden
rice.tips: Season A (Sep–Feb) rains give the best yields in Rwanda's marshlands.

maize.requirements: Well-drained loamy soil · Temp 18–27°C · pH 5.8–7.0 · 500–800 mm rain
steps: Deep-till soil to 30 cm; incorporate compost or crop residue
Plant seeds 3 cm deep, 30 cm apart in rows 75 cm apart
Apply DAP (50 kg/ha) in the planting furrow at sowing
Top-dress with CAN or urea when plants are 30–40 cm tall
Weed thoroughly at weeks 2 and 6 after planting
Scout for fall armyworm; spray approved pesticide if larvae found
Harvest when husks are dry and brown; dry cobs to 13 % moisture before storage
maize.tips: Intercrop with beans to naturally boost soil nitrogen and reduce risk.

kidneybeans.requirements: Fertile well-drained soil · Temp 16–24°C · pH 6.0–7.0 · Moderate moisture
steps: Prepare loose, well-drained soil; avoid heavy clay or compacted ground
Plant seeds 4 cm deep, 20 cm apart in rows 60 cm apart
Apply DAP at a low rate (25 kg/ha) in planting furrow
Water regularly during flowering and pod-fill stages
Weed at weeks 2 and 5; mulch to conserve soil moisture
Monitor for bean stem maggot and angular leaf spot disease
Harvest when 90 % of pods are dry and seeds rattle inside the pod
kidneybeans.tips: Rotate with maize each season to prevent disease buildup in the soil.

blackgram.requirements: Well-drained light soil · Temp 25–35°C · pH 6.0–7.0 · Warm dry climate
steps: Prepare well-drained, light soil; avoid heavy clay and waterlogged spots
Plant seeds 3–4 cm deep, 20 cm apart in rows 45 cm apart
Apply phosphate at planting; skip nitrogen — crop fixes its own
Irrigate lightly once a week during extended dry spells
Weed at weeks 2 and 5 after planting
Monitor for bean fly and yellow mosaic disease; remove infected plants
Harvest when 80 % of pods turn black; dry and thresh
blackgram.tips: High-protein pulse — good for food security and natural soil improvement.

lentil.requirements: Well-drained silt-loam · Temp 7–27°C · pH 6.0–8.0 · Cool highland climate
steps: Deep-till and prepare a fine seedbed; remove stones and large clods
Inoculate seeds with Rhizobium; plant 4 cm deep, 10 cm apart in rows 30 cm
Apply phosphate fertilizer at planting; nitrogen inoculation replaces N-fertilizer
Water sparingly — lentils prefer cool, slightly dry highland conditions
Weed early at weeks 2 and 4; lentils compete poorly against weeds
Monitor for Ascochyta blight and aphid colonies
Harvest when lower pods turn yellow; dry whole plants then thresh
lentil.tips: Best suited to highland areas above 1700 m elevation in Rwanda.

banana.requirements: Deep fertile well-drained soil · Temp 15–35°C · pH 5.5–7.0 · Year-round moisture
steps: Dig holes 60×60×60 cm; place 10 kg compost at the bottom
Plant healthy sword suckers 3 m apart in each direction
Apply thick mulch (15 cm) around each plant to conserve moisture
Fertilize with NPK (200 g per plant) every 3 months
Remove all suckers except one chosen follower per plant
Prop tall plants with bamboo stakes to prevent wind toppling
Harvest when fingers fill out and color starts to change (9–12 months)
banana.tips: Keep only one follower sucker per stool to ensure continuous production.

mango.requirements: Deep well-drained soil · Temp 24–30°C · pH 5.5–7.5 · Dry flowering period
steps: Dig a 1 m × 1 m × 1 m hole; fill with topsoil and 20 kg compost
Plant grafted seedlings 10 m apart at the start of rains
Water twice weekly for the first 6 months; reduce once established
Apply NPK (300 g) three times per year for young trees
Prune annually to shape the canopy and remove dead branches
Monitor for mango seed weevil and anthracnose; spray at fruit set
Harvest when fruits change color and yield slightly to gentle pressure
mango.tips: Grafted trees produce fruit faster and yield better than seedling trees.

watermelon.requirements: Sandy to loamy soil · Temp 22–32°C · pH 6.0–7.0 · Warm dry sunny weather
steps: Prepare raised beds 30 cm high; incorporate compost and coarse sand
Plant 3 seeds per hole, 1.5 m apart in rows 2 m apart
Thin to the strongest 1–2 plants per hole after germination
Apply NPK at planting; side-dress with nitrogen at vine formation stage
Water deeply once a week; reduce watering as fruits begin to ripen
Hand-pollinate early morning if bee activity is low; control aphids
Harvest when the tendril near fruit dries and the belly spot turns yellow
watermelon.tips: Use drip irrigation to keep leaves dry and prevent fungal disease spread.

orange.requirements: Deep well-drained soil · Temp 15–35°C · pH 5.5–7.0 · Year-round moisture
steps: Dig a 1 m × 1 m × 0.6 m hole; fill with topsoil and 15 kg compost
Plant certified grafted seedlings 6 m apart at the start of rains
Irrigate regularly for the first 2 years; apply heavy mulch around tree
Apply citrus NPK fertilizer every 3–4 months throughout the year
Prune annually to remove dead wood and inward-crossing branches
Monitor for citrus psylla and greening disease; remove infected trees
Harvest when fruits turn orange-yellow and taste sweet (3–5 years)
orange.tips: Only buy certified grafted planting material — it is disease resistant.

papaya.requirements: Fertile well-drained soil · Temp 22–32°C · pH 5.5–7.0 · Wind-sheltered site
steps: Dig 60×60×60 cm holes; fill with compost and topsoil mixture
Plant 3 seeds per hole, 2.5 m apart; thin to 1 plant after germination
Identify sex at flowering (4–5 months): keep 1 male per 10 females
Apply NPK monthly for first 6 months, then bi-monthly thereafter
Mulch heavily around each plant; water twice weekly in dry season
Remove plants infected with ring spot virus immediately
Harvest when one side of the fruit turns yellow (6–9 months)
papaya.tips: Replant regularly — productive economic life is only 3–4 years.

coconut.requirements: Coastal sandy soil · Temp 27–32°C · pH 5.0–8.0 · High humidity, lowland
steps: Select healthy, fully mature seed nuts; germinate in nursery for 3–6 months
Plant seedlings in 1 m × 1 m × 1 m holes spaced 8 m × 8 m apart
Apply 10 kg compost and 500 g NPK per tree twice per year
Irrigate during dry season; ensure drainage is good in the wet season
Remove old fronds regularly to reduce rhinoceros beetle habitat
Use pheromone traps to monitor and control rhinoceros beetle
Harvest when outer husk changes color — nuts mature in 4–6 years
coconut.tips: Coconut grows best below 400 m elevation; mulch heavily to retain moisture.

coffee.requirements: Deep volcanic soil · Temp 15–24°C · pH 6.0–6.5 · Highland, dry flowering period
steps: Plant in 60×60×60 cm holes, 2.7 m × 2.7 m apart; add shade trees alongside
Apply thick mulch (coffee husks or grass, 15 cm) around each plant
Fertilize with NPK (200 g) at the start of rains; top-dress with urea mid-season
Prune to a single stem for the first 2 years; then maintain 3–4 main branches
Monitor for coffee berry borer; use BGI traps and frequent cherry picking
Hand-pick only fully ripe red cherries for Fully Washed premium quality
Pulp, ferment, wash, and dry cherries on raised drying tables
coffee.tips: Join a cooperative to access premium Rwanda Fully Washed coffee prices.

avocado.requirements: Deep well-drained fertile soil · Temp 15–30°C · pH 5.5–7.0 · Sheltered site
steps: Dig holes 1 m × 1 m × 1 m; fill with 20 kg compost and topsoil mixture
Plant grafted seedlings 8–10 m apart at the start of the rainy season
Water twice weekly for the first 6 months until the tree is established
Apply NPK (200 g per tree) three times per year for the first 3 years
Prune to remove crossing branches and maintain an open, sunlit canopy
Monitor for anthracnose and root rot; avoid waterlogged soils
Harvest when fruits reach full size and skin color begins to change (3–5 years)
avocado.tips: Hass variety performs best in Rwanda's mid-altitude areas above 1400 m.

cassava.requirements: Sandy loam to loamy soil · Temp 20–35°C · pH 5.5–7.0 · Drought tolerant
steps: Prepare a deep, well-tilled seedbed; remove stumps and large stones
Cut healthy stems into 25–30 cm stakes; plant at 45° angle, 5–10 cm deep
Space stakes 1 m apart in rows 1 m apart for optimum canopy cover
Apply DAP (50 kg/ha) at planting; top-dress with urea 3 months after
Weed thoroughly at weeks 4 and 10 — cassava is sensitive to early competition
Monitor for cassava mosaic virus (yellow mosaic pattern); remove infected plants
Harvest when lower leaves yellow and roots are 3–5 cm thick (9–12 months)
cassava.tips: Always use certified virus-free planting material to prevent cassava mosaic disease.

potato.requirements: Fertile well-drained loam · Temp 10–22°C · pH 5.5–6.5 · Highland 1500 m+
steps: Prepare a well-tilled, ridged seedbed; incorporate 10 t/ha of organic compost
Plant certified seed tubers 30 cm apart in furrows 75 cm apart, 10 cm deep
Apply DAP (200 kg/ha) in the planting furrow at the time of planting
Earth up (hill soil around stems) when plants reach 20–25 cm height
Top-dress with CAN or urea 6–8 weeks after planting
Scout weekly for late blight; spray with approved fungicide at first sign
Harvest when vines die back naturally; dig carefully to avoid bruising tubers
potato.tips: Rwanda's highlands provide ideal cool temperatures for premium potato production.

sorghum.requirements: Wide range of soils · Temp 20–35°C · pH 5.5–7.5 · Very drought tolerant
steps: Prepare a firm, well-tilled seedbed; sorghum germinates best in warm, moist soil
Plant seeds 3–5 cm deep, 15–20 cm apart in rows 60–75 cm apart
Apply DAP (50 kg/ha) at planting; top-dress with urea at 30 cm plant height
Thin to one strong plant per hill two weeks after emergence
Weed at weeks 3 and 6; sorghum competes poorly with weeds early on
Monitor for sorghum midge and striga weed; use resistant varieties where possible
Harvest when grain is hard and moisture content drops below 20 %
sorghum.tips: Preferred in dry areas of Rwanda — sorghum yields where maize often fails.

soybean.requirements: Well-drained fertile loam · Temp 20–30°C · pH 6.0–7.0 · Moderate moisture
steps: Prepare a well-drained, fine seedbed; avoid compacted or waterlogged soils
Inoculate seeds with Bradyrhizobium bacteria immediately before planting
Plant seeds 3–4 cm deep, 5–10 cm apart in rows 45–60 cm apart
Apply phosphate fertilizer (DAP 50 kg/ha) at planting; skip nitrogen
Keep soil moist at flowering and pod-fill stages — these are critical periods
Weed at weeks 2 and 5; monitor for pod-sucking bugs and leaf rust
Harvest when 90 % of pods turn brown; thresh promptly to avoid shattering
soybean.tips: Inoculate seeds with Bradyrhizobium to maximize nitrogen fixation and yields.

sweetpotato.requirements: Sandy loam soil · Temp 21–27°C · pH 5.5–6.5 · Moderate moisture
steps: Prepare loose, ridged beds 30 cm high; incorporate compost for better drainage
Plant healthy vine cuttings (30–35 cm) horizontally into ridge tops
Space vines 30 cm apart in rows 90 cm apart; press firmly into soil
Apply potassium-rich fertilizer (MOP 60 kg/ha) 4 weeks after planting
Weed at weeks 2 and 5 before vines cover the ground
Lift a few roots to check size at 3 months; harvest all before rains return
Cure harvested roots in shade for 5–7 days to harden skins before storage
sweetpotato.tips: Orange-fleshed varieties are vitamin A-rich and fetch premium prices in Rwanda.

tea.requirements: Acidic loamy soil · Temp 13–25°C · pH 4.5–6.0 · Highland 1500 m+, high rainfall
steps: Choose a highland site with reliable 1200 mm+ annual rainfall and no waterlogging
Plant rooted cuttings 1.2 m apart in rows 1.5 m apart at the start of rains
Apply CAN (150 kg/ha) at planting; acidify soil with sulfur if pH exceeds 6.0
Mulch heavily with grass or pruned material to retain moisture and suppress weeds
Prune to a flat table (plucking table) at 60 cm height in year 3
Harvest (pluck) the top two leaves and bud every 7–14 days during the growing season
Deliver fresh leaf to the factory within 6 hours of plucking to preserve quality
tea.tips: Rwanda's highland tea commands premium prices — join a factory cooperative for best returns.

tomato.requirements: Fertile well-drained soil · Temp 18–27°C · pH 5.5–7.0 · Sheltered, sunny site
steps: Raise seedlings in a nursery for 3–4 weeks; harden off before transplanting
Transplant seedlings 45 cm apart in rows 75 cm apart into ridged beds
Install stakes or cages immediately after transplanting
Apply DAP at planting; top-dress with CAN every 3 weeks during growth
Tie stems to stakes as they grow; remove side shoots (suckers) weekly
Irrigate at the base — keep leaves dry to prevent blight and Septoria leaf spot
Harvest when fruits are firm and fully colored; pick every 2–3 days
tomato.tips: Stake plants early and remove suckers weekly — this doubles yield per plant.

wheat.requirements: Well-drained loamy soil · Temp 10–22°C · pH 6.0–7.5 · Cool highland climate
steps: Deep-till to 25 cm; apply 5 t/ha of compost and incorporate before planting
Broadcast or drill seeds at 120 kg/ha; cover with 3–5 cm of soil
Apply DAP (100 kg/ha) at sowing; top-dress with urea at tillering stage
Weed carefully at 3 and 6 weeks — wheat loses yield quickly to competition
Scout for stem rust and yellow rust; spray with approved fungicide at first sign
Avoid excessive nitrogen late in the season — it increases lodging risk
Harvest when grain is hard and moisture is below 14 %; thresh and dry promptly
wheat.tips: Best suited above 1800 m in Rwanda's cool highland zones; use certified rust-resistant seed.
